t: Connecting a tvOS app to other devices over the local network
a: Display a view in your tvOS app that lists available iOS, iPadOS, and watchOS devices that the user can connect to over their local network.
Specifies the access level requested for device discovery.
A UIKit view that displays and manages the device discovery and pairing process.
A UIKit view that displays other devices on the network, and creates an encrypted connection to a copy of your app running on that device.
Display an interface that lets people connect iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS, and Mac Catalyst apps over peer-to-peer networks.
A control that allows a user to become discoverable and advertise to local devices.
A SwiftUI view that displays other devices on the network, and creates an encrypted connection to a copy of your app running on that device.
An environment value that indicates whether the current device supports device discovery.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the current device supports device discovery.
Use the system’s default access for the device selected by the user.
A network connection endpoint for the device that a person selected.
Creates a `DevicePairingView` which, when pressed, will display a local device advertiser interface.
Creates a view that displays the available devices with the access level, section handler, and other parameters you supply.
Creates a view that displays available devices.
Creates a view controller that displays the available devices on your local network.
Creates a view controller with the parameters and access level you specify that displays the available devices on network.
Initializes a device-pairing view controller with the provided listener and requested access level for device discovery.
Creates a view controller that displays the other, available devices on your local network.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the current device supports device discovery using Wi-FI Aware.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the current device supports device discovery.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether the current device supports device discovery.
Grant the app permanent access to the device selected by the user for future use.
Sets the completion handler that the system calls when the user selects a device from the picker view.
Configures the view after the framework loads the view controller’s view into memory.
